top10.name: Broken Access Control
top10.description: Failures typically lead to unauthorized information disclosure, modification, or destruction of all data.

top10.name: Cryptographic Failures
top10.description: Failures related to cryptography (or lack thereof), which often leads to sensitive data exposure or system compromise.

top10.name: Injection
top10.description: Injection flaws, such as SQL, NoSQL, OS, and LDAP injection, occur when untrusted data is sent to an interpreter as part of a command or query.

top10.name: Insecure Design
top10.description: Focuses on risks related to design flaws. A secure design requires secure architecture/patterns and threat modeling.

top10.name: Security Misconfiguration
top10.description: Security misconfiguration is the most commonly seen issue. This is commonly a result of insecure default settings, incomplete or ad hoc configurations.

top10.name: Vulnerable and Outdated Components
top10.description: Components, such as libraries, frameworks, and other software modules, run with the same privileges as the application.

top10.name: Identification and Authentication Failures
top10.description: Authentication and session management are often implemented incorrectly, allowing attackers to compromise passwords, keys, or session tokens.

top10.name: Software and Data Integrity Failures
top10.description: Relates to code and infrastructure that does not protect against integrity violations. An example is an application that relies upon plugins from untrusted sources.

top10.name: Security Logging and Monitoring Failures
top10.description: Without logging and monitoring, breaches cannot be detected. Attackers rely on this to maintain persistence and extract data.

top10.name: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF)
top10.description: SSRF flaws occur whenever a web application is fetching a remote resource without validating the user-supplied URL.
